summary: Admin access restriction feature validated. Backend tests: 8/8 passed (admin login/non-admin rejection for login_role=admin, is_admin flag on /auth/me, RBAC on /admin/users, /account/profile). Frontend verified: admin user (ronraj2312@gmail.com) sees Admin Center & Account Details in sidebar; non-admin user sidebar hides those links; /account redirects unauthenticated users to /login. One minor UX issue found.

ui_bugs.issue: When non-admin user attempts admin login, backend returns 403 with detail 'This account does not have admin access. Only approved administrators can sign in here.' but UI only displays a generic 'Connection error' message. The specific error detail should be shown to the user.

frontend: Admin login via Internal Access > Admins panel succeeds, redirects to /dashboard
Admin sidebar shows data-testid=nav-admin (Admin Center) and data-testid=nav-account (Account Details)
Clicking Admin Center navigates to /admin and renders Admin Center UI (Operations/Module Access/Admin/Employees tabs visible)
Clicking Account Details navigates to /account (not blocked for admin)
Logout via user-menu-trigger + logout-btn works and redirects to /login
Direct navigation to /account for unauthenticated regular user redirects to /login (ProtectedRoute guard enforced)

action_items: [LOW] Improve error message surfacing on Login form (Internal Access > Admins): display backend's 403 detail message ('This account does not have admin access...') instead of generic 'Connection error' when non-admin tries admin login.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/admin.py redefines require_admin and require_super_admin locally (lines 38-48), shadowing the versions imported from shared.py. The shadowed require_admin in admin.py uses role-only checks and does NOT call is_approved_admin()/check user_type=='internal'. This is currently OK because CENTRAL_ADMIN_EMAILS are auto-promoted to master_admin role, but it is inconsistent with shared.is_approved_admin() which also accepts user_type='internal' without an admin role. A plain 'internal' user without admin role would be rejected by routes/admin.py's local require_admin. Recommend removing the local overrides in routes/admin.py and relying on the shared import.
routes/admin.py also redefines log_audit_action locally (lines 51-63) - duplicated logic. Remove in favor of shared.log_audit_action import.
routes/auth.py redefines hash_password/verify_password locally (lines 128-132) even though they are already imported from shared.py. Remove duplicates.
Frontend login error handling: when API returns 403 with a detail message, the UI collapses all errors into a generic 'Connection error'. Wire the response.data.detail into the form error state.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_admin_access_restriction.py (new pytest suite for admin access restriction feature)

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (admin flow fully verified; non-admin sidebar hiding verified by count=0 assertion in automated run; non-admin login form UX uses generic error)

seed_data_creation: Created regular user random_test_user_999@example.com via POST /api/auth/register (idempotent in test fixture).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Feature fully working at API level (8/8 pytest). Admin access restriction uses shared.is_approved_admin() which accepts: user_type=='internal' OR role in [admin, super_admin, master_admin] OR email in CENTRAL_ADMIN_EMAILS. login_role='admin' in POST /api/auth/login enforces this. Minor UX gap: frontend login form shows 'Connection error' instead of the backend 403 detail text when non-admin tries admin login. routes/admin.py has duplicate local definitions of require_admin/require_super_admin/log_audit_action that shadow shared.py imports - consider cleanup (non-blocking).
rca of the issue: N/A - no blocking issue. The feature is implemented correctly end-to-end. is_approved_admin() in shared.py correctly gates admin access. Frontend Sidebar.jsx correctly conditionally renders Admin Center and Account Details using user.is_admin. ProtectedRoute fetches /auth/me on every render to get fresh is_admin. Only gap is cosmetic error message display on failed admin login from non-admin.
